channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: OIL TOP CONFIRMED — 46 replies — 184.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
OIL TOP CONFIRMED

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-03-09 21:52 UTC

OP

WHALE OPENED GIGASHORT ON OIL. EVERYTHING WILL BE FINE AND OIL IS GOING DOWN.

we're good, oil ain't going any lower. buy shorts on monday for free money.

Early replies (first 3)

61953753
>>61953747
wait this was on the 7th. fuck.

61953772
>>61953747
>Entry
>91.431
RIP Bozo. Bobo deserves to lose money.

61953786
I dont short thoughbeit

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61954011
>>61953747
He voted for it.

61954033
>>61953792
>my mom
The most reliable of sources

61954037
>>61953747
Where do they find this information?

61954038
>>61953747
>800k margin position
>trading oil on a crypto exchange
literally a small fry in the world of commodity trading.

61954136
>>61953944
So it could just be a hedge and not an attempt at profit.

61954466
>>61953987
>destroys your entire argument by masturbating in public

61954473
>>61953747
ICEsisse cope!
EVChuds WON

61954504
>>61953934
>turning off a transponder makes your giant tanker ship invisible to drones

61954524
>>61953747
>120 usd/barrel
kek why are people panicking? that's not even close to the biggest jump in oil prices

61955056
Welcome to /pmg/ fuckers. It was a bank just shorting to suppress the spot price. It happens to bullion at 8am est daily and 5pm est Sundays

61955095
>>61954524
bro we're at 114 right now

61955194
>>61953747
What? $3 million position in oil isn't that the equivalent of me opening a $10 position in Nvidia?

61955211
>>61953747
KWABOTY

61955266
top reached

61955307
Looks like he was wrong.

Huh this dumbass thread was only 3 hours ago? Feels longer.

61955510
>>61955266
I just made 60,000 dollars on oil futures and I'm going to have 250k by the end of the week while you keep calling tops that never come.

61955569
>>61955056
/pmg/ here, you are fucking retarded. Do you really think a 2.8M short can have any impact on the largest commodity market in the world lol? Oil is the cornerstone of modern civilization, it's not something you can tank with a fucking 3M bet on binance lol. There is $30M worth of oil futes changing hands every second.

61955988
>>61953944
>Equivalent of 24 future contracts
>Whale
AHAHAHAHAHAHAHAHA
You can trade that all in one trade in one contract on one exchange and it wouldn't even register.
Daily Volume in COMEX WTI is like 3 million contracts.
>>61953813
If he gambled 280 million instead of 2.8 he'd move a whopping 0.07% of daily volume. Only on one exchange and one contract, of course.

61955997
>>61953904
They will pay you to store it in a shed you own? I could fit 4 barrels in my tool shed.

61956086
I might throw a little bit into USO, nothing I would get salty about losing if things reversed.

61956162
>>61953747
This one will be wiped out. Always the overly eager bears. Not time yet.

61956284
>if oil pumps to 120/barrel he'll be wiped out

61956296
>>61953747
is that the US government/FED? imagine them getting liquidated, would be funny

61956352
HOLY SHIT LMFAO

INVERSE CRAMER WORKS

HAHAHAHAHA OIL WENT DOWN

61956490
>>61956352
Countries are beginning to unload their oil reserves. If the strait is still closed when they run out then... well...

61956500
Crude tickling the 200ma bounce

61956512
>>61955510
Pressing X

61956748
>>61953747
>3 million
>whale

61959846
>>61956490
Patriots are in command

61960726
>>61953747
Damn, he never misses, does he?

item.title: Certified Banger Money Farming — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Certified Banger Money Farming

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-03-09 21:53 UTC

OP

What are some methods, from which you have actually seen returns from, to make money outside of a day job
Side hustles, beer money, whatever you wanna call it, just name some good grinds out there that you could recommend to basically anyone
>Uhhhh just invest or hedge against some shitcoin
Not the point of the thread fuck off!
Mostly online methods preferred but not necessary (Yes I am aware some things take physical effort)
If it can be done both on a phone and PC even better
Personally I have been trying to form my own hustle for a little over a month now and have a subscriber base totaling $100 a month, not bad but nothing major

Early replies (first 3)

61952894
>>61952873
Indoor shrimp farming + Garloid wrangling

61952930
>>61952873
knee pads

61952932
>>61952930
overused joke

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61953415
Gold farming in osrs. elite void + scobo and you can start making bills.

61953746
>>61953415
big brained

61953781
>>61952894
>Indoor shrimp farming

61953950
>>61953781
this is goku on namek

61955477
>>61952873
Also looking for stuff

61955575
>>61952873
>>61955477

picrel

61955582
>>61955575
It ain’t a real video breh

61955605
>>61952873
Paid clinical trials are the ultimate truth nuke

61957064
>>61955605
What about plasma

61957251
>>61952873

You gave me false hope for Youtube with your thumbnail

61957258
>>61952873
pokemon investing

61957496
>>61957251
Sorry breh

61958438
>>61952932
But enough about your mom.

61960733
>>61958438
woah

item.title: NUCLEAR HAPPENING — 7 replies — 20.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
NUCLEAR HAPPENING

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-03-09 21:34 UTC

OP

BITCOIN IS PUMPING

Early replies (first 3)

61956725
>>61956520
Kek baggies.

61957228
>>61956520
>40% down
fucking retards

61957267
The ta guy i follow got like 10 calls right in a row and his smugness is due for a smiting

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61957295
>"pumping" after falling 60+K.

Not falling for it again.

61957329
How is this possible with the orangutan destroying the world's discretionary income?

61957340
>>61956520
time to short

61960591
>>61956520
Link is at $9 again. So BTC will dump. Short it for free money

item.title: American war just began. Long oil, short BTC bros — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
American war just began. Long oil, short BTC bros

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-03-09 21:50 UTC

OP

American war just began. Long oil, short BTC bros

Early replies (first 3)

61960712
>The people of Havana didn't revolt
>Carpet bomb them
really?

61960713
>>61960680

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: WTF — 33 replies — 80.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
WTF

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-03-09 21:30 UTC

OP

YOU SAID TO BUY OIL YOU SAID IRAN WAS GOING TO CLOSE THE STRAIT OF HORMUZ FUCK ALL OF YOU

Early replies (first 3)

61959820
The usa ran out of weapons lol. the strait is still fucked.

61959822
>>61959817
I didn't say that, you listened to someone else, should have listened to me retard

61959823
No refunds my brown friend

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61959829
>>61959820
most of their bases and missile defense systems got blown up, that's why they're out.

61959833
>>61959817
Iran is a complete paper tiger. No IRGC troop is going to risk his life at sea when 90% of them are only there for a paycheck. The ones that are willing and qualified to do it are dead

The only way they could close the strait would be to mine it, but you would just end up with operation praying mantis 2

61959855
>All of /biz/ thinks they are getting rich buying oil after it goes up 20% in one day
Absolute Top Signal, you abject retard

61959864
>buying oil
You were supposed to do that during the obviously failing negotiations.
You were then supposed to sell it precisely last night.
In the long run you could still buy oil companies but they're trading at a 10% premium to where I personally think they should be so good luck lmao.

61959880
trump realized israel was about to ruin his entire legacy with an endless sand war and snaked them to negotiate a deal with iran

61959887
>>61959833
>No IRGC troop is going to risk his life at sea when 90% of them are only there for a paycheck.

I think you're describing american troops.

61959897
>>61959820
cope

61959929
>>61959817
1 NOTHING WRONG WIth me.

61959956
>>61959887
True, but they are statistically way less likely to die, and aren’t being forced to attack oil tankers on speed boats like somalian pirates

61959957
What happened why did it correct so hard?

61959970
>>61959957
Trump is pulling a MISSION ACOMPLISHED

61959988
>>61959817
Turns out the US Military annihilated the IRGC in a week flat.

61960005
>>61959957
It's not public but some tankers are getting through the strait and reports are getting around the shipping and oil industries. Insurers will get pressure to insure from both industry and from the government side going forward; maybe the US will do some reinsurance under the table.

Basically it's not a total oil shutdown of the middle east like some feared.

61960025
It will pump back up, it dropped a little because the G7 is CONSIDERING using its strategic reserve

Buy the dip NOW NOW NOW

61960033
>>61959817

Only took threatening 400m SPR release and trump literally saying “the war is over very soon”, aka, everything they fucking have.

You and every little retard with 15k in their RH accounts want to go long equities SO BAD. I can’t wait to watch you get wiped out. Today we learned that 120 oil is the global “panic” number. Let’s see how many days it’ll take to get there again

61960048
>>61960025
Not a bad strat just set your sell targets and stop losses and expect to close positions in a timeframe of days rather than weeks. Volatility is high but it won't stay high for very long, things will eventually shake out and the systems will be adjusted (drilling, refining, transport routes, etc). US oil will almost certainly be exported to Europe more; so US oil prices will probably stay high.

61960057
>>61960033
>global panic number
>markets down 2% from ATH

You third worlders are massive retard

61960096
Oil is now behaving like a memecoin and zigzagging over engagement farming xitter accounts. I think we're fine, honestly.

61960148
>>61960057

lil bro? Do you even trade stocks or just come here for the epic memes?

61960153
it'll zoom back up

61960156
>>61959855
>posts a bear
But people bullish on oil are already bearish about the economy.

61960182
>>61960033
Probably not too long desu. It was said last week that oil could go to $150 if this lasts as long as people say it will. I managed to wake up at 10:15 this morning and sold everything near the top but if it drops further the next day or two I might put a few thousand back in. I originally bought June calls thinking this would slowly grind up.

61960199
>>61960153
Oh my god… it is fucking over…

61960239
>>61960199
>what is a refinery?

61960280
>>61959957
Trump basically declared victory and said the "war is complete", i.e he's looking for a way out despite calling for Irans unconditional surrender yesterday.

The markets are coping that everything will suddenly go back to normal.
Because surely both Israel and Iran will now stop attacking eachother and get along, and insurance premiums in the gulf states will surely go down knowing Iran can shut down the strait any time and the US will be hesitant for another prolonged conflict..

61960393
>>61959897
nice regime change, cowards. another L for the books

61960418
why do you listen to these fucks, when they say "death to America" that's your clue that's these are unserious people. and that's who runs and populates this board by a wide margin

61960453
>>61960280
Israel is out of interceptors, they don't have a choice but to stop if the US pulls out.

61960510
>>61959817
USA gave up faster than we thought. My bad. I underestimated TACO

61960559
>>61959833
Iran is a Russia/China proxy. We are using all of our missiles in Ukraine/Iran, taking interceptor missiles out of Asia to blow up shitty Chinese drones(assembled in Iran). China is going to make a play on Taiwan soon, the defense industry is too corrupt to increase production. Saw this writing on the wall when Israel started spraying round-up on Syrian fields last year. Trump can’t back out, Israel is giving him a BJ and treating to bite down if he does.

item.title: WFH CHADS REPORT IN — 60 replies — 69.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
WFH CHADS REPORT IN

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-03-09 21:02 UTC

OP

Let's all laugh at the wagies who have to pay insane amounts for gas to get to work kek

Early replies (first 3)

61953886
>>61953855
Imagine spending half your days pay worth of money just to get to work and back lmao

61954053
>>61953855
antisemitic wfh antizog antimiga chads will inherit the earth

61954077
>>61953886
>half
You mean full for minimum wage chads, don’t forget about rent and bills too.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61958588
>>61953855
WFH is true king status. Sometimes I fuck my wife on the clock. I've had her under my desk sucking my dick while on a teams meeting. So good.

61958609
>>61954954
Job market is brutally bad right now. That makes us folks who have good WFH jobs even bigger Chads. And yea, fake jobs out the ass, AI recruiters, AI interviews, fake shit all over.

61958767
i work from home everyday and i get paid well.
problem is that my apartment has become my prison. i have become more agoraphobic than over and depression is creeping in. my portfolio is doing good though.

61958794
>>61958767
Go take a walk for 15 minutes whenever you feel that. Always cures it for me

61959001
>>61953855
What field do you faggots work in where wfh jobs fall from the sky like rain?
And don't fucking say tech cuz every tech role near me is fully onsite

61959006
>>61958588
I remember fucking my gf on my lunchbreak once lol it was a good memory

61959021
>>61959001
If you have the skills, any job will allow you to work from home that doesn't have a specialized worksite (Lawyer::Courtroom, Soldier::Iran, Tradesman::Jobsite)

61959146
What WfH jobs are there where you basically don't do shit? Or if you have work, no one will know if it's completed or not

61959227
>>61953855
At the end of the day, we're still wagies. I want to die, I hate this. I don't want to work!
t. have 2 WFH jobs at once, still not enough, still do doordash after work for some extra cash. Made $115k in 2025

61959302
>>61959227
>still not enough
You might be in a similar boat as me, perhaps slightly financially irresponsible? I work one job for the same amount, but didn't listen and lived above my means with the nice amount of money. Start living below your means, spend less, pay off the debt and you should be able to breathe. Making over 100k we shouldn't be suffering
>as if I'm going to listen to this as well kek

61959314
>>61958588
This is literally how I got my now my wife pregnant. Be careful. I was having too much careless sex.

61959328
>>61959146
You need to actually work. WFH isn’t magic free money

61959331
>>61959302
I can definitely admit to spending a bit too much but the main problem is that I have a family. Wife and now 2 kids (my son was born in January). Wife does not work, so I pay all the bills. I was also super irresponsible before I got into this position so I had a lot of debt and that's pretty much where most of the extra money is going. I have 5k credit card debt left to pay off, slowly but surely I'm almost there. But things would be moving faster if I either made more money, or if my wife worked as well.
I am literally getting ready to apply for a third WFH job just to try and get myself over that threshold so I can start to really save and get out of debt faster. My biggest spending is on food, I love to eat out, it's my only joy in this horrible existence beyond my kids.

61959344
>>61953855
Jokes on you I drive an electric car.

61959354
>>61959328
>You need to actually work
Ok what WfH jobs require the least effort

61959370
>>61959354
That really depends on the company anon. The easiest is any kind of support desk position, but depending on the company, you could be super busy. Usually, the ones that keep you barely busy will have you do auxiliary shit to make up for your lack of busyness, so you're always going to have some level of work you don't wanna do.

I have 2 WFH jobs because one of my jobs is a lot less involved, they don't monitor you, barely on the phone (mostly emails) but technically I should be doing more (writing articles/making tutorial videos) and I'm just lazy and/or distracted by other job which needs you to be infront of the laptop almost the entire time (phones, emails and chat), hard to get away from it, and they track the shit out of your metrics. I hate it, but the second one is what gives me health insurance and other benefits.

61959379
>>61959331
literally me except no family, damn I'm sad

>>61959354
Being a leader/manager in something. I work in finance and am a low level manager. I started as a nobody on the phones and worked my way to that. I work at home half the time, but I'm gaming 95% of the time when I'm at home. When I'm in a meeting I make sure to not be the presenter that way I can just talk/listen while playing games. The saddest part is I'm still more productive than most people. Us gaming while working is the equivalent of women walking around with StarBucks and talking about nothing all day at work... jk we still do more than they ever would

61959385
>>61953855
AI is going to make everyone return to office.

61959429
>>61959385
???

61959568
>>61958767
go do the work at coffeeshop meme. parks are nice too. my favorite is work from ski lodge

61959577
Well. My wife still goes into jewish daycare every day, so we are still spending money on gas.

61959674
>>61954808
Also accountant here. How easy was it to get a wfh position? What industry? What state?

61959787
>>61954808
Also accountant here. How easy was it to get a wfh position? What industry? What state?

61959961
Anyone got their job recently? Almost landed a wfh gig but after the fourth technical interview they closed the job. It's pretty insane. In the past a phone screen and one interview was all it took. Now you have every humiliation ritual in the book just to get the chance of something

61959986
>>61959961
It’s been that bad for at least ten years. Even before AI, HR was still filtering you with algorithms

61960001
>>61959986
It was way easier in 2021-2024. I had a few remote jobs at once before getting cut back to 1. They let me go due to budget cuts.

61960254
>>61959674
>Also accountant here. How easy was it to get a wfh position? What industry? What state?
>How easy was it to get a WfH position?
I think the job listing wasn't listed as WfH but I ask every interview.
>What industry?
Healthcare management - it's not in healthcare I have several facilities I manage & will be getting more which is why I want a new job
>What state
Sorry don't want to give this

The funny thing is this position asked for like 1-3 years experience but they've taken people fresh out of college and let them WfH
Ideally I want a Gov WfH job but I have no idea if my state even offers remote positions for gov jobs and they take a fucking long time to get back to you, I applied back in December to one and it's still in the pipeline, only 1 update

61960265
>>61960254
I'm a retard
>What industry
It's not in a hospital I meant, I manage several healthcare facilities
Pay is significantly better than what I got paid at my shitty small CPA practice with my cunt post-menopause boss but it's really not a great position

61960308
>>61959961
I'm just applying to everything now, got an assessment with the National Safety Council, they've got a couple different positions open but mostly just looking through support related jobs on LinkedIn. If I nail a $40,000+ a year position that will bring me back to six figures.

61960341
I make 250k a year WFH and I barely do any work. Is anyone else absolutely loving life right now?

item.title: AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHHHHH — 106 replies — 56.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHHHHH

Open thread • Replies: 106 • Updated: 2026-03-09 20:02 UTC

OP

FUCKING OIL IS $104!!!!!!!!!
+14%%%% AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAHHH

Early replies (first 3)

61953190
this man is on suicide watch >>61950166

61953192
>>61953183
my honest reaction
>how the fuck is it still below $200?

61953223
> bobo at his nephews 9th birthday over the weekend

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61956494
>>61954845
Let Israel deal with the consequences of their own actions?

61956501
>>61956494
They dont deal in the real world, anon

61956502
>>61956442
>>61956392
>>61956488
I think it's time to wait until Euros start trading.

61956504
107$pls, i want off this wild ride

61956506
>>61956478
>>61956428
>>61956378
Doing this is admitting the war is gonna keep going for weeks and getting into uncharted territory.

61956516
>>61953270
Methamphetamine

61956524
>>61953183
>he boughted at $120

61956532
Isn't it too early to use reserves?

61956551
>>61956532
look at this shit. Oil is literally used in everything from energy, to getting the goods you buy to the store or people to there jobs

61956558
Everybody has become such a gambler that they're adding shorts left and right. Monday will end with oil at 120-130.

61956565
>>61956478
>speculation
So when they don't release it, we go back up.

61956566
>>61956532
I think they are betting on the war ending soon. In which reality do these people live?
Does everyone actually believe that the orange monkey nigger will magically end the war in two weeks?

61956635
did they released cum reserves already? why drop to 100 ?

61956651
>>61956635
Not yet, G7 will be discussing it today.

61956656
>>61953183
What part of 'short term pain for long term gain' don't you retards understand? The big guy's got a plan. All this market noise is generated by libtards to make him look bad. I feel bad for you retards for thinking these squiggly lines on your screen are real and mean something.

61956675
>>61956566
that's what they're being told
and they will listen lol

61956683
>>61956532
reserves can be replaced

61956915
>>61955845
Curse those liberals for going to war with Iran for Israel

61957031
>>61956635
What's the matter? You didnt happen to bet against America, did you?

61957087
>>61956566
The west has been mindbroken by peace for so long that they can't fathom a war that last multiple years anymore unless it is some cold conflict against an insurgency.
It's like how people thought WW1 was going to last a couple months because there hadn't been a major war in Europe for a hundred years aside from the Prussians sealclubbing the Austrians and French when they were weak.

61957147
>>61956463
Same, at least makes me feel better that I wasn't the only one.

61957315
>>61956463
That's how the silver fomos feel who bought at $113.

61957348
>>61953183
chill
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=eVTXPUF4Oz4

61957485
>>61956463
115 here

61958040
>>61955248
Unironically yes. Libtards in California/bongistan/Germany are getting rekt by high oil prices. They taxed the shit out of their gasoline to the point that people could barely afford it and the oil price spike is devastating them.

61958585
>>61957087
It's nothing new. During the first battle of Bull Run civilians took picnic baskets to watch the war like it was a sports game then got trampled to death during the retreat. When a generation forgets the horrors of war this cycle just repeats over and over.

61958597
The US is a net oil exporter, so yay?

61959480
>>61955978
250 cents

61959838
It's going to end up lower than this morning jej

61959894
>>61956488
I saw this faggots post during the pump and knew something was up

item.title: GO BANKRUPT FOR ISRAEL!!!! GOYIM!!!!! DJT — 172 replies — 42.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
GO BANKRUPT FOR ISRAEL!!!! GOYIM!!!!! DJT

Open thread • Replies: 172 • Updated: 2026-03-09 17:54 UTC

OP

GO BANKRUPT FOR ISRAEL!!!!

GOYIM!!!!!

DJT

Early replies (first 3)

61954017
>>61954004
BIDENS OIL

61954029
>>61954004
that kid should have practiced his aim a bit more

61954034
>>61954004
I voted for this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61957233
>>61954938
slaughtered

61957235
>>61957200
refusing to take the L is not the same as winning

61957296
>>61957229
>America will be fine for the most part its us in Europe and Koreans/japanese
i say this as a europoor but i think we have a better shot than the US, but only really because i think the US is so incredibly badly mismanaged right now. biden admin could have probably done the same broad strokes as trump but accomplished them much more competently.

u guys will probably hate the comparison but it looks like ww2 pre-war german/italian shenanigans except now it's US that's impossible to work with due to retarded leadership. if you're a random country like canada or whatever, you're probably more likely to want to integrate more with EU than the US or china at this point. it's clear US has given up on soft power and is trying to just strong-arm countries directly (which doesn't really work if you're trying to do it to everyone at once)

>unless everyone plans a coordinated attack on the US dollar
pretty sure china is basically already trying to by not buying bonds anymore but idk what exactly it would take (getting rid of the petro dollar?)

61957307
>>61954970
If they had nukes kikemerica and israhell wouldn’t have boomed them and possibly caused another refugee crisis

61957310
>>61954004
What a grammatical mess, what an absolute retard.

61957320
oil is linked to the price of diesel
diesel is linked to the price of food
◇ food is linked to the price of McChickens
no one will ever vote republican again

61957326
>>61954867
>Destroying Israel is going to be the best thing for humanity on the long run, the cost is nothing in comparison.

FTFY

61957361
>>61954140
First, the orange pedo has never read a book, doesn't read period, and is too much of a narcissist and an idiot to ever go "hmm, maybe I don't understand this, time to listen to people who are smarter than I am". Second, people around him are all sycophants, retarded influencers, religious fanatics, or terminal cowards. Third, MAGA as a whole abhors the idea of expertise, humility, knowledge, accountability, and professionalism - all the things that are critical for statesmanship.

Buckle up, because this is only gonna get worse.

61957387
>>61956282

item.title: How high will OIL go? — 42 replies — 36.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
How high will OIL go?

Open thread • Replies: 42 • Updated: 2026-03-09 20:46 UTC

OP

150?
200?

Early replies (first 3)

61950050
>>61949961
The Trump admin probably starts trying to implement price controls above $150.

61950059
>>61949961
10,000 Gold.

61950077
How will crypto perform without rate cuts in the next 3 years?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61950847
>>61950801
My math was for the US only. As the world's largest producer of oil and now an oil exporting country, we have it better than most other countries.

61950857
>>61949961
No one cares about oil prices anymore, chud.

61950868
>>61950790
Driving a little gasoline Toyota Corolla 100,000 miles @ 40mpg and $5.80/gal: $14,500

Driving a larger electric Toyota bZ4X 100,000 miles at 252 miles per charge and $15/charge: $5,952

Look at what happened to the motor vehicle market after the 1973 and 1979 oil crises. Definitely not a nothingburger for investors.

61950872
>>61950857
Didn't Tesla stop making cars?

61950875
>>61950872
>tesla is the only ev maker
>>61950868
>still not owning an ev

61951080
>>61950184
>non-working radars
Funny that you say that

61951096
>>61949961
What oil stock do I buy bros????

61951236
>>61951080
Here is a real image, unlike your lazy well poisoning attempt
https://edition.cnn.com/2026/03/05/middleeast/radar-bases-us-missile-defense-iran-war-intl-invs

61951283
>>61949961
What will happen on Monday bros..?

61951306
>>61951283
Oil go up

61951325
>>61949961
At this point, over 300. Short term I'm guessing around 150.

61951346
>>61949961
I still don't understand why Americans give a blood sacrifice to Israel every 10 years. Is it some sexual thing?

61951350
>>61949961

This is the biggest oil production cut in the history of mankind. I don’t think an initial spike to $300 before face melting deflation kicks in is out of the question

61951441
>>61951346
It is a sexual thing and it's on tape.

61952245
>>61950875
>still not owning an ev

In the 90s and 2000s my dad was 100% against cell phones, despite cell phones having been around in some form since the 70s. He grudgingly finally got one for business around 2010. Now he doesn't have a home landline. You'll huff and puff for another decade while the world moves on from internal combustion vehicles, but you'll eventually own one like everyone else. Every oil crisis has accelerated the replacement of less efficient vehicles with more efficient vehicles, and we've maxxed out the efficiency of gasoline engines. New battery technology is already doubling range and longevity while halving charge time over what was possible just a couple of years ago. People are running out of excuses to avoid EVs and reasons to keep ICEs. So, you can be the modern equivalent of a guy in the 1930s still riding a horse into town and rejecting those new fangled automobiles, but from an investor standpoint EVs and the technology surrounding them are where the growth is and will continue to be. Personally, I look forward to a world without jeet-owned gas stations and white trash greasemonkey youtube slop.

61952254
>>61951441

oh vey

61952315
>>61952245
Yeah you want overpriced golf carts with planned obsolescence instead. Such an improvement!

61952559
>>61950713
>>61950728
Based and rare high analysis anon, Im doing an investigation currently myself but it’s difficult without drawing from past source’s because the current MSM story is far removed from reality even from a cursory glance. Need to keep eyes on the situation as it unravels, but it seems you’re indicating that this is more than a short spike and could be a year long event?

61954746
>>61950868
Spend an extra $20,000 on a car to save $8548 on gasoline

yore a geneos dude

61955625
>>61949961
it was about -$40 a few years ago so I'm guessing it might reach $3.50 soon

61956311
>>61954746
The nice thing about gasoline cars is that you can "recharge" in less than 5 minutes AND have the ability to double your range simply by carrying extra gas cans.

Electric takes HOURS to charge, and you can't exactly take an extra battery with you.

It's a matter of convenience, and then there is the big brother IoT tracking your every move, which electric vehicles will surely have in them + hardwired kill switches. But that's getting a little off in the weeds.

Combustion engine vehicles are just simply better, more reliable, more convenient.

61956709
slurp the dip?

61957060
>>61952559
>but it seems you’re indicating that this is more than a short spike and could be a year long event?

1. Oil extraction is already shutting down or gas been shut down in the regions that ship oil through the strait.

2. Oil storage in these regions is maxxed out or about to be maxxed out.

3. It takes a couple of weeks to get production going again.

4. It takes a few weeks to queue up tankers, fill them, have them travel halfway around the world, and offload their oil.

5. It takes maybe a week to refine that offloaded oil and have it ready for distribution.

6. Countries like Japan are already tapping their strategic reserves, which will divert some of that oil.

So, if the war ends tomorrow and speculators leave the oil market, fuel prices will still remain elevated due to simple supply constraints for *at least* 6 to 8 weeks. Realistically, the war is going to last at least that long and the strategic reserves of various countries will need even more refilling. We're probably looking at 6 months of high gas prices *at least*.

7. Unlike 1973, the Saudis and a couple of other countries aren't cutting production but increasing it. So prices will likely stabilize at an elevated level soon(ish).

8. Unlike 1979, the US is now a net exporter of oil and oil extraction has increased around the world thanks to new technologies. There are many more vehicles in the world, but the global motor pool is also significantly more efficient than in 1979/80.

So, high fuel prices are going to last awhile, but probably won't get as high as they would if we were facing the exact same situation as in '73 or '79.

61957226
>>61956311
So you drive 300 miles to work or something?

61957526
>>61950050
Then the sellers will just stop shipping and production.
It will go over $200.

61957540
$250 and momentary spike to $800 at some point.

61957556
All markets at this point are skewed by massive retail gambling, you're going to get burned if you chase price action.

61960238
>>61957556
that gap is going to get filled, back to the 80s we go

61960246
>>61949961
50 by EOM

item.title: The US has 10 days — 170 replies — 27.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US has 10 days

Open thread • Replies: 170 • Updated: 2026-03-09 15:47 UTC

OP

The US has 10 days to clear the strait of Hormuz or global markets will crash (not a hyperbole)

At first, markets brushed off the conflict as another 12 day war. However, realization is setting in that Iran is actually capable of deterring passage through the Hormuz strait by 90%.

The problem is that every day that goes on is the equivalent of shutting off 10+ billion barrels of oil every single day. As storage becomes full, producers are forced to shut in production, which takes 2 weeks to restart. Thankfully, most of the world is fine for 1-2 weeks without gulf oil. After that, problems begin.

Oil will reprice a geopolitical premium and $100 is LOW for the magnitude of production shutdown.

What happens: bond yields will roar to new highs as a huge wave of inflation begins. All rate cut probabilities get removed, and you will see the traditional end of the business cycle, high oil prices will turn growth into recession for most economies. None of this is priced in equities. And the longer it goes on, the higher likelihood of it happening.

How to end it? Trump has fallen for a Trump. There is no way to clear irans fast mountain ranges littered with AD and missile silos, thus tankers will keep getting shot in the Hormuz strait. The only way is to put troops on the ground.

Don’t really care if you think I’m a permabear or a doomer, I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in. They don’t understand that this is an existential war for Iran, it’s not some “geopolitical dip”.

Pic is Asian jet fuel prices

Early replies (first 3)

61938343
>>61938341
Slurp XOP? Wut do

61938344
So should I sell my TLT?

61938345
>>61938341
>I’m just giving you a warning that for whatever reason, equities don’t want to price in
Trump spent all of last year insider trading and TACOing so he's not trustworthy anymore

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61956110
>>61938402
Nah you can launch shaheads from the back of a ute, I've seen it done
Also, USVs do not require any launch platform at all and have proven devastatingly effective in the black sea - though with that said I have yet to see Iran or its houthi proxies deploy those in any serious number

61956116

61956309

61957832

61957835

61957837

61957854
>>61938341
Rolling that this war goes on for years months and the global economy shits itself.

61958442
>>61957018
>a hyperbole
You're retarded. Hyperbole is a noun and you are too used to retarded internet speak that uses it as an adjective (which is hyperbolic).

Furthermore, dismissing a message because of grammatical or linguistic issues you find in the message is retarded. Even if "hyperbole" were an adjective, were you really unable to understand the meaning of his message? Language is about transacting messages/meaning. If the message was delivered, that is all that matters.

t. engineer working on semantic compression method for agent-to-agent communication

Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
